formal_definition: HM Treasury, the UK government department that made the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Cryptoassets) Regulations 2026 (SI 2026/102) on 4 February 2026 under its FSMA order-making powers, that assesses and recognises stablecoins as systemic to UK payments for the purposes of Bank of England regulation under Part 5 of the Banking Act 2009, and that is running the pilot issuance of a Digital Gilt Instrument (DIGIT).
plain_definition: Short for HM Treasury, the UK government department responsible for financial-services policy -- broadly the equivalent of Hong Kong's FSTB. It made the Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (Cryptoassets) Regulations 2026 as a statutory instrument, decides whether a stablecoin is systemic enough to be regulated by the Bank of England rather than the FCA, and is running its own Digital Gilt Instrument (DIGIT) pilot.
